item.title: MMAE: A Massive Multitask Audio Editing Benchmark
item.description: 📄 New in cs.SD, cs.CL — We introduce MMAE, a Massive Multitask Audio Editing benchmark, serving as the first comprehensive evaluation testbed designed for general-purpose instruction-based audio editing. Spurred by the shift toward intelligent creation, interactive editing has rapidly expanded from visual domains, pione...

item.title: UniSHARP: Universal Sharp Monocular View Synthesis
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — In this work, we focus on extending SHARP, the popular photorealistic view synthesis method, for universal monocular rendering across a continuum of camera systems, from conventional perspective cameras to wide-field-of-view, fisheye and omnidirectional panoramic settings. To overcome the pinhole...

item.title: Watch, Remember, Reason: Human-View Video Understanding with MLLMs
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV, cs.AI — Video understanding is being rapidly transformed by multimodal large language models (MLLMs), as research moves from short clips to long, multimodal, and knowledge-intensive video scenarios. These scenarios require models to handle sparse evidence, long-range dependencies, multimodal alignment, a...

item.title: PaperFlow: Profiling, Recommending, and Adapting Across Daily Paper Streams
item.description: 📄 New in cs.IR, cs.AI — Scientific paper recommendation is typically evaluated as static ranking over a fixed candidate set, yet real scientific reading unfolds as a daily, longitudinal process in which interests shift and feedback accumulates. We introduce PaperFlow, a framework that organizes it into three coupled sta...

item.title: Your UnEmbedding Matrix is Secretly a Feature Lens for Text Embeddings
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL, cs.IR — Large language models exhibit impressive zero-shot capabilities across a wide range of downstream tasks. However, they struggle to function as off-the-shelf embedding models, leading to suboptimal performance on massive text embedding benchmarks. In this paper, we identify a potential cause under...

item.title: Streaming Video Generation with Streaming Force Control
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — We introduce StreamForce, a streaming video generation framework that enables physically grounded control through continuous force inputs. Unlike prior video models that train separate models for different force types, assume fixed forces, or rely on non-causal processing, StreamForce is a causal...

item.title: Socratic-SWE: Self-Evolving Coding Agents via Trace-Derived Agent Skills
item.description: 📄 New in cs.SE, cs.AI — LLM-driven software engineering agents have become a central testbed for real-world language-model capability, yet their training remains limited by the availability of high-quality SWE tasks. Existing synthetic data methods typically create tasks through fixed mutation or bug-injection procedure...

item.title: Andyyyy64 / whichllm
item.description: 💬 Active HN discussion (68 comments) · ⭐ 103 GitHub stars today — Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real, recency-aware benchmarks, not parameter count. One command, run it instantly.

item.title: mvanhorn / last30days-skill
item.description: ⭐ 1.1k GitHub stars today · ⭐ 33.0k total stars — AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web - then synthesizes a grounded summary

item.description: ⭐ 579 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 84.3k total stars — TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework

item.title: google / skills
item.description: ⭐ 481 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 12.1k total stars — Agent Skills for Google products and technologies

item.title: Panniantong / Agent-Reach
item.description: ⭐ 961 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 23.6k total stars — Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.
